metadata.name: IПƬӨ ƬΉΣ VӨID #7
metadata.description:
Na rotina amassada, minha mente esgotada produz esse pequeno vazante de cores no seu claustro — É por onde escoa o resto de minha essência.

A psicodelia deve ser exercitada, mas quando em prol da labuta, torna-se mais uma obsessão do homem por aquilo nada psicodélico — a posse.

Suspire, aceite a simplicidade do subjetivo e desfrute do seu momento de calma.

The music was produced by @GrooveIury and the code by @nt_worm.

Korg Synth, VolcaSample, VolcaBass and Minilogue were used
through Tascam Porta07 (tape recorder).

Developed using Hydra and Web Audio Api.

Click to get started.

Your machine's performance may affect the reproduction of this work - optimized for Chrome on Desktop.

metadata.name: Returns #420
metadata.description:
Returns is an offline-first PWA and an interactive SVG. It combines the joy of interaction, digital color and minimalist sculpture with the potential of a long running, continuously unique visual. Based on “Shipping” from 2016, it is inspired by Anne Truitt, Ugo Rondinone, Imi Knoebel, Kelly Ellsworth and many others.

A subdivision slices the viewport influenced by width, height and depth. Corners are cut by replacing them with two new points, pulled towards each other along the edges. Shapes are “rounded" by repeating this step, keeping the new points close together. As colors change, so do the relations between shapes, even if geometry changes at a slower pace. New compositions are created on click or after a timeout. This progress over time allows for infinite iterations within the space of each token. It is the promise of a seeded starting point, plus the potential for unlimited new and unique iterations and states over time.

metadata.name: The Differential #64
metadata.description:
Noise forms along a crystal edge; points delineate the surface in a thousand clustered moments, each one its polar opposite, joined and viewed from a distance by separate, sylph-like fragments of an instant's slow tick.

At the event horizon, the escape velocity is equal to the speed of light. Since general relativity states that nothing can travel faster than the speed of light, nothing inside the event horizon can ever cross the boundary and escape beyond it, including light.

metadata.name: Hexaëder #125
metadata.description:
Hexaëder (cube) is a research into abstraction and a search for a balance between simplification and visual complexity.

No matter how simple and unambiguous things appear to be, in reality they are often more complex. Enjoy the simple, explore the complex.

Hexaeder has a wide variety of possible outcomes. I have put together 10 different color palettes; the number of cubes ranges from 10 to 401 (18 different grids); each cell can have 4 different background patterns; each cube can be filled in 4 distinct ways; there are 5 line colors and 16 different "cube figures"; the vignette can be black or be the color of the palette, and there are several "texture settings". This results in over 1.8 million possibilities.

For more information see: https://www.behance.net/gallery/131114761/Hexaeder-Flattened-cubes
